sections.paragraph:
σπαράσσω 1 aor. ἐσπάραξα. Pass.: aor. 3 pl. ἐσπαράχθησαν 2 Km 22:8; pf. ptc. nom. pl. fem. ἐσπαραγμέναι 3 Macc 4:6 (orig. ‘tear, pull to and fro, rend’; Aristoph., Ran. 424 τὰς γνάθους; Diod S 8, 32, 3 and 19, 34, 3 τὰς τρίχας; Chariton 3, 10, 4 τὰς κόμας; Jos., Ant. 11, 141 τὴν κεφαλήν; Da 8:7 ἐσπάραξεν αὐτὸν ἐπὶ τὴν γῆν.—The word in another sense goes back to Aeschyl. [Prom. 1018, Zeus will shatter a jagged prominence]; also PPetr II, 17, 4, 2; 6; LXX, Philo) τινά someone (Aristoph., Acharn. 688 ἄνδρα σπαράττων καὶ ταράττων; s. Herodas 5, 57 ‘handle roughly’; JosAs 12:10; Jos., Ant. 13, 233) shake to and fro an unclean spirit convulses the person in whom it dwells (ἄνθρωπος σπαραττόμενος of an attack: Cyranides p. 59, 15) Mk 1:26; 9:20 v.l.; Lk 9:39. W. acc. of pers. to be supplied Mk 9:26.—B. 566. DELG. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural
  2. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

fem. fem. = feminine

orig. orig. = original(ly)

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Chariton Chariton , scriptor eroticus (writer of romances) I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

PPetr PPetr = The Flinders Petrie Papyri—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Cyranides Cyranides , a compilation relating to magical properties of stones, plants, and animals; perh. antedating I/II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 936
